originalTitle: Women secretly filmed, then ridiculed and abused online
title: Let's Be Safe and Kind Online!
content:
Hello, Super Kids of Africa!

Welcome, amazing friends! Today, we have a very important story to share with you. It's about how we can all be safe and kind when we use the internet. The internet is a wonderful place, full of games, learning, and fun, but it also has some rules to keep us safe, just like when you play outside.

What Happened to Some Ladies?

In two beautiful countries in Africa, Kenya and Ghana, something happened that made some ladies feel very sad. A man made videos of them, like short movies. He did this when they did not know he was filming them. Then, he put these videos on the internet for everyone to see. He did not ask the ladies if it was okay to film them. And he definitely did not ask if it was okay to share their videos online. This made the ladies feel upset and worried. Their pictures and videos were shared without their permission. This is not a kind thing to do. **Did you know?** Kenya and Ghana are two wonderful countries in Africa, known for their beautiful wildlife and friendly people! Many of you live in amazing African countries too!

What Does "Asking First" Mean?

Let's talk about something super important: "asking first." This is also called "permission" or "consent." Imagine you have a favorite toy, like a special ball or a doll. If your friend wants to play with it, what do they do? They ask you, "Can I please play with your toy?" Right? If you say "yes," then it's okay for them to play. If you say "no," then they shouldn't play with it. It's your toy, and you get to decide. Asking first means you get to choose what happens with your things. It means getting someone's "yes" before you do something that involves them or their special belongings, like their pictures.

Our Pictures, Our Rules!

Your pictures and videos are like your special toys. They belong to you! They are part of who you are. Nobody should take your picture or make a video of you without asking you first. And nobody should share your pictures or videos online without your clear "yes"! It makes us feel safe and happy when people respect our choices. When someone shares your picture without asking, it can make you feel surprised, confused, or even sad. Everyone has a right to decide what happens to their own image. This is called privacy. It means keeping some things just for yourself or for people you choose. **Did you know?** Your smile is unique and special! Only you get to decide who sees your pictures and videos online.

Being Kind Online

The internet is like a really big, busy playground. There are so many exciting things to see and do! But just like in a real playground, we must always remember to be kind to everyone. We should use our good manners online too. We should not say mean things in messages or comments. We should not share pictures or videos that make other people sad, embarrassed, or uncomfortable. Being kind online means treating others with respect, just like you would want to be treated. It means thinking before you click, type, or share. Let's all be good online friends and make the internet a happy place for everyone.

What If Something Makes You Sad Online?

Sometimes, even when we try to be careful, things might happen online that make us feel not so good. If you ever see a picture or video online that makes you feel scared, sad, or worried, what should you do? You must tell a grown-up right away! This is the most important thing to remember. Tell your mom, your dad, your grandma, your grandpa, your teacher, or any trusted adult. They are there to help you and keep you safe. It is very brave to tell a grown-up when something is wrong or makes you feel bad. They can help fix the problem and make sure you are safe again. **Did you know?** Telling a grown-up when something worries you is a superpower! It helps keep you and others safe.

Important Rules for Being Safe Online

Here are some super important rules to help you stay safe and have fun when you are online: **Rule 1: Always ask a grown-up.** Before you go online to play games or watch videos, always ask your mom, dad, or another grown-up first. They can help you find safe things to do. **Rule 2: Don't share your secrets.** Never tell strangers your real name, where you live, your phone number, or the name of your school. Keep your personal information private. **Rule 3: Don't talk to strangers.** If someone you don't know tries to talk to you online, don't answer them. Tell a grown-up immediately. **Rule 4: Don't open strange things.** Never click on links or open messages from people you don't know. They might not be safe. **Rule 5: Be kind!** Always use kind words and be respectful to others online. Don't say anything online that you wouldn't say to someone's face. **Rule 6: If it feels wrong, tell!** If anything you see or hear online makes you feel scared, sad, confused, or uncomfortable, tell a grown-up right away. They will help you. These rules are like your safety shield for the internet! They help you enjoy all the good things online while staying protected. **Did you know?** The internet connects people all over the world, even across big oceans and continents, like from Africa to many other places!

Why Is This So Important?

Everyone, especially children like you, deserves to feel happy and safe. No one should be made fun of or made to feel bad online. That is not fair. Taking pictures or videos of people without asking is not kind. Sharing them without their "yes" is also not kind. We want the internet to be a place where everyone is treated with respect and where everyone feels good. Just like in your school or your home, we want everyone to feel safe and valued. Learning these rules helps us all build a better, kinder, and safer online world for children everywhere, including all the wonderful kids in Africa.

Let's Remember to Be Good Online Friends!

So, remember our important lessons today, super kids! Always ask first before taking or sharing pictures and videos. Always be kind with your words and actions online. And always, always tell a trusted grown-up if something worries you or makes you feel unsafe online. Your safety is super important to us! Let's all work together to make the internet a happy and safe place for all kids in Africa and all over the world!
summary: Some women in Kenya and Ghana had their videos secretly taken and shared online without their permission, reminding us all that we must always ask first and be kind when using the internet to keep everyone safe and happy.
whyMatters: It's super important because everyone has a right to feel safe and happy online, and we need to learn to ask for permission before sharing pictures or videos, and always be kind to others' feelings when we use computers or phones.

originalTitle: Iran’s football team honours children killed in Minab school airstrike
title: Football Heroes Remember Children
content:
Hello, Future Superstars!

Hi everyone! It's your friendly kid-friendly news reporter from Africa here. I have a very special story to share with you today. It's a story about kind hearts, football players, and remembering children. It comes from far, far away, but it shows us that kindness and love are everywhere, for all children.

Football is a Wonderful Game!

First, let's talk about football! Do you love kicking a ball? Football, also called soccer, is a game where two teams try to kick a round ball into a goal. It's super fun to watch, and even more fun to play! Players run very fast and work together as a team. Football players are often like heroes to many children all around the world. They wear special jerseys and score amazing goals! It takes lots of practice and teamwork to be a great football player. Many kids dream of playing football when they grow up. Did you know? Football is the most popular sport in the whole world! Billions of people love to play or watch it!

Meet the Iranian Football Team

Our story is about a football team from a country called Iran. Iran is a country very far away from us, in a place called Asia. These players are very good at football. They train hard and play exciting matches. They were getting ready to play a game in another country called Turkey. Turkey is a beautiful country that is also very far from us. It is a place where Europe and Asia meet, making it very special. Did you know? Turkey is famous for its beautiful places like ancient castles and yummy food, like sweet Turkish Delight!

A Very Sad Story

While the football players were in Turkey, they heard some very, very sad news. It was about children in a town called Minab, back in their home country, Iran. Minab is a place where children, just like you, go to school every day. Children go to school to learn their ABCs, to count numbers, to draw colourful pictures, and to play with their friends. School is a very important and safe place for all children. It’s where they grow and learn about the world. But then, a very loud and scary thing happened near the school in Minab. It was like a big boom from the sky. This very sad and scary event hurt the school. And, very sadly, some children who were at school lost their lives. Sometimes, when loud noises from the sky hurt places, people call it an 'airstrike.' It's a very bad thing that makes everyone very sad and scared. It's important for children to always be safe, especially at school, which should be a place of fun and learning, not fear.

A Special Way to Remember

The football players heard this sad news. Their hearts felt very heavy for the children and their families. They wanted to do something special. They wanted to show everyone that they remembered these children. They wanted to show that they cared very, very much about what happened. They wanted to send a message of love and kindness. So, what did they do? They thought of a beautiful and quiet way to remember. They gathered on a big green football field. This was not for playing a game. Instead of footballs, they brought something else. They brought something that children use every day.

Backpacks on the Pitch

They laid school backpacks on the grass. You know, just like the backpacks you carry to school every morning? Each backpack was carefully placed on the green field. They put many backpacks down, one for each child who was hurt in that sad event. Did you know? Your backpack helps you carry your books, pencils, and lunch! It helps you get ready for a day full of learning and fun at school! When the players placed the backpacks, it was a very powerful moment. Each backpack was like a silent friend, remembering a child who should have been at school. It was a way of saying, "We wish these children were still here, carrying their bags, learning, and laughing."

Players with Big Hearts

The football players stood very quietly around the backpacks. They bowed their heads. You could see the sadness and care in their faces. This was their way of showing respect and love for the children they had never even met. It showed how much they value children's lives. They showed the world that even strong, famous football players have very big and kind hearts. They used their voices and their actions to say: "Children everywhere deserve to be safe. Children everywhere deserve to go to school and learn, without any fear." Did you know? Many football players around the world use their fame to help others, like building schools or helping children in need!

What is a 'Tribute'?

When someone does something special to remember people, especially after something sad happens, it's called a 'tribute.' It's a way to show respect, honour, and love. It tells the world that these people are important and will not be forgotten. The football players' act with the backpacks was a very beautiful and heartfelt tribute. It was a silent message that spoke very loudly to everyone who saw it. Did you know? A tribute can be a special song, a quiet moment of silence, or even planting a tree to remember someone important and special.

Why This Story Matters

This story matters to all of us. It reminds us that children, no matter where they live in the world, are very important. Every child deserves to feel safe and happy. Every child deserves to learn and play without any fear, especially at school. When people, like these football players, show kindness and remember others, it makes the whole world a better place. It helps us all think about how we can be kinder to each other every day, and stand up for what is right. Did you know? Around the world, there are many kind people and organisations working hard to make sure all children can go to school safely and learn amazing things!

Let's Dream of Peace

This special act also makes us all dream of peace. Peace means no scary loud noises like the one in Minab. Peace means no fighting. Peace means everyone can play safely in their homes and at their schools, no matter where they live. Let's hope all children, everywhere, can live in a world full of peace and happiness. Always remember to be kind to your friends, your family, and everyone around you. Because kindness makes our world brighter for all children, everywhere! Stay safe and keep shining, future superstars!
summary: Iranian football players put school backpacks on a field in Turkey to remember children who sadly lost their lives in a very sad event near a school in Minab, showing love and care for all children.
whyMatters: This story is important because it shows how people can care about others, even far away, and reminds us that all children deserve to be safe and happy at school.

originalTitle: Israelis question Lebanon war as opposition grows
title: People in Israel Ask Big Questions About Helping Their Country!
content:
Hello from Africa!

Hi everyone! It's your friendly reporter from Africa here, ready to share some news with you. I hope you are having a super day! Today, we are going to talk about something happening in a country far away, called Israel. It's a bit like a big puzzle, but we will put the pieces together, just for you! Imagine you live in a big, friendly neighbourhood. Sometimes, things happen that make people wonder and ask questions. That's what's happening now, and it's okay to ask questions. It helps us learn and understand the world better. Let's find out more together!

What is Happening?

Far away, in a country called Israel, people are talking a lot. They are asking big questions about what their country is doing. Their country has a group of grown-ups called the government. The government is like the 'big brothers and sisters' who help make rules and plans for everyone in the country. Did you know? Israel is a country in a place called the Middle East. It's not too far from our big continent of Africa! Sometimes, countries have problems with their neighbours. It's a bit like when you and your friend might disagree on something. In this case, Israel has a neighbour country called Lebanon. Sometimes, there is fighting between countries. When there is fighting, it can make people very sad.

Neighbours, Like You and Me!

Think about your house. You have neighbours living next door, right? Well, countries also have neighbours! Israel and Lebanon are two countries that are neighbours. They share a border, which is like an invisible line on the ground that shows where one country stops and another begins. When countries are neighbours, it's really important for them to get along. Just like you want to play nicely with your friends next door! But sometimes, things get a bit tricky. Did you know? Africa has 54 countries! That's a lot of neighbours living together on one big continent! There has been some fighting near the border between Israel and Lebanon. This fighting makes people in Israel think a lot. They are asking their government, "Is this the best way to keep us safe?" They want to make sure everyone is safe and happy.

Asking Big Questions

It's okay to ask questions, isn't it? When you're in school, if you don't understand something, you put up your hand and ask your teacher. That's a very smart thing to do! It helps you learn. Well, in Israel, many people are doing something similar. They are asking questions to their government. They want to know more about the plans their government has for the fighting near Lebanon. They want to understand why things are happening the way they are. This is called "raising questions" or "growing opposition." It just means more and more people are thinking and asking. They are not saying the government is bad, but they want to make sure the plans are the very best plans for everyone. Did you know? Asking questions helps us learn and make good choices! It shows we care. These questions are about the "military strategy." That's a big word, isn't it? It just means 'the plan for how the soldiers will act.' Soldiers are brave people who help protect their country. So, people want to know if the plan for the soldiers is the best one to keep everyone safe and stop the fighting quickly.

What is a Government?

Let's talk a little more about the 'government'. Imagine you have a team, like a soccer team or a school team. You have a captain or a leader who helps make big decisions for the whole team, right? The government is like the leader of a country's big team. They are a group of grown-ups chosen by the people to make important decisions. These decisions are about how the country works, like building roads, making schools, and keeping people safe. They try their best to help everyone. When people in Israel are asking questions, they are talking to their government. They are saying, "Hey, we trust you, but can we talk about this plan and make sure it's the best one for all of us?" Did you know? In many countries, people get to choose who is in their government by voting! It's a special way for grown-ups to have a say.

Why Do People Ask Questions?

People ask questions for many reasons. Sometimes, they might be a little bit worried. They might worry about their friends or family who live near the fighting. They might worry about their homes. Asking questions also shows that people care deeply about their country. They want what is best for everyone. They want peace, which means no fighting, just everyone living happily together. Imagine if you and your friends were building a very tall tower with blocks. If someone sees that a block is a bit wobbly, they might ask, "Is that block in the right place? Maybe we should move it so the tower doesn't fall down!" That's like what people are doing in Israel. They are trying to help make sure their country is strong and safe, like a very strong block tower.

Wishing for Peace

No one likes fighting. It's sad when people argue or when there is fighting between countries. Everyone, everywhere, wishes for peace. Peace means being able to play, go to school, and sleep safely in your bed without any worries. When people in Israel ask questions about the fighting, it's often because they want to find the best way to bring peace. They want the fighting to stop so everyone can be safe and happy again. They want to protect their homes and their families. Did you know? The word 'peace' is important in many languages around the world, like 'salaam' in Arabic and 'shalom' in Hebrew!

Let's Learn Together!

So, what have we learned today? We learned that in a country called Israel, people are asking their government about the plans for fighting near their neighbour, Lebanon. They are asking questions because they care about safety and want the best for their country. It's a way for people to talk to their leaders and make sure everyone is thinking about the best ways to solve problems. Remember, it's always good to ask questions if you don't understand something. It's also good to hope for peace and kindness everywhere in the world, including between neighbours and countries.

Our Message of Hope

From all of us here in Africa, we send our best wishes to the children and families in Israel and Lebanon. We hope that the grown-ups can find the best ways to talk and solve problems peacefully. Every child, everywhere, deserves to live in a world filled with peace, laughter, and friendship. Keep shining bright, and keep asking questions, because that's how we learn and grow!
summary: People in the country of Israel are asking their government important questions about how they are handling some fighting with their neighbour, Lebanon, because they want everyone to be safe and find the best way to have peace.
whyMatters: It matters because when people ask questions, it helps leaders make good choices for safety and peace, so everyone can live happily without fighting.
